perturbation.original_text: A. You will pay us without offset, credit or deduction of any nature, so long as this Agreement is in effect, a monthly Continuing Fee equal to five percent (5%) of the Gross Sales derived from the Franchised Restaurant.
perturbation.changed_text: A. You will pay us without offset, credit or deduction of any nature, so long as this Agreement is in effect, a monthly Continuing Fee that can be adjusted at our discretion between five percent (5%) and ten percent (10%) of the Gross Sales derived from the Franchised Restaurant.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the continuing fee from a fixed 5% to a discretionary range (5%-10%), we create uncertainty about the franchisee's financial obligations. While section X.A originally clearly stated the fee, the modification gives the franchisor leeway to increase it, which contradicts the initial agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. We must receive from you within five (5) days after the end of each month, a correct statement of your Gross Sales for the preceding fiscal month as determined by us on a form approved by us and signed by you. Each monthly statement of Gross Sales shall be accompanied by the Continuing Fee payment based on the Gross Sales reported in the statement so submitted. You will make available to us all original books and records that we may deem necessary to ascertain your Gross Sales for reasonable inspection at reasonable times.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a fixed Continuing Fee of 5%. The changed text introduces a discretionary range (5%-10%) for the Continuing Fee, which contradicts the contradicted text. The contradicted text states that the Continuing Fee payment should be 'based on the Gross Sales reported in the statement so submitted.' With the changed text, the Continuing Fee is no longer directly and predictably based on the Gross Sales alone, since it can be adjusted within the 5%-10% range at the franchisor's discretion. This introduces uncertainty and a direct conflict regarding how the fee is calculated and paid, constituting a strong in-text contradiction because the franchisee cannot determine the exact fee based on the stated Gross Sales.

perturbation.original_text: B. You have the right to renew the franchise for two (2) successive terms equal to five (5) years each, providing you meet all of the following conditions:
perturbation.changed_text: B. You may have the right to renew the franchise for two (2) successive terms equal to five (5) years each, providing you meet all of the following conditions:
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'You have the right' to 'You may have the right' in the term and renewal section introduces uncertainty. This makes the renewal contingent on unspecified factors beyond just meeting the listed conditions, contradicting the original implication of guaranteed renewal upon fulfillment of the conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. You have, during the entire term, complied with all the provisions of the Agreement;
perturbation.justification: The original text states the franchisee 'has the right' to renew if conditions are met, creating a reasonable expectation of renewal upon fulfilling those conditions. The changed text uses 'may have the right,' introducing uncertainty and making renewal discretionary, even if conditions are met. This directly contradicts the clause stating that compliance during the entire term is a condition. By making the right to renew uncertain even with compliance, the changed text creates a contradiction because compliance was initially presented as a pathway to guaranteed renewal.
